candidate.title: Balatro

source_refs.title: Balatro store page
source_refs.notes: Primary reference for the game candidate.

candidate.short_description: Poker-inspired roguelike deckbuilder built around familiar card semantics.
candidate.selection_reason: Strong sample for transforming familiar rules into systemic depth at low art cost.

profile.one_sentence_summary: A poker-hand roguelike deckbuilder where score multipliers, not direct combat, drive a snowballing run.
profile.core_loop: Play poker hands, buy and arrange jokers that rewrite scoring, then beat escalating blind targets.
profile.progression_model: Run-based economy where the player spends winnings on jokers and upgrades between blinds.
profile.failure_model: Failing to reach a blind's score threshold ends the current run.
profile.content_structure: Procedural run over a fixed escalating ladder of blinds with shop phases between them.
main_player_actions: select cards to form a poker hand
buy jokers and consumables in the shop
main_player_decisions: which poker hand to commit to this round
which joker synergy to invest in
when to reroll or save money
innovation_patterns: familiar rules as an on-ramp to systemic depth
scoring engine as the core toy
non_replicable_risks: depth depends on a large tuned joker set

evidence.title: Balatro presentation
evidence.quote_or_summary: The game communicates almost entirely through cards, text, and numbers rather than animation.

evidence.title: Design interpretation
evidence.quote_or_summary: Joker combinations create deep emergent scoring strategies.

evidence.title: Balatro design summary
evidence.quote_or_summary: Starts from recognizable poker hand logic and layers score modifiers over it.

claims.subject: Balatro
claims.explanation: Players already understand poker hands, so the systemic depth layers onto known rules instead of new ones.

evidence.title: Balatro design summary
evidence.quote_or_summary: Recognizable poker hands give an immediate rules vocabulary before modifiers add depth.

claims.subject: abstract card UI
claims.explanation: Card, text, and number feedback avoids animation and bespoke art, lowering production load for a small team.

evidence.title: Design interpretation
evidence.quote_or_summary: Pure card and number presentation needs no animation pipeline.
